debug_info.goal:
V : Type u_1
Ω : Type u_2
m : MeasurableSpace Ω
G H : Ω → SimpleGraph V
p q : ↑unitInterval
P : Measure Ω
hG : @SimpleGraph.IsBinomialRandom V Ω m G p P
hY : @SimpleGraph.IsBinomialRandom V Ω m H q P
⊢ AEMeasurable (fun ω => G ω ⊓ H ω) P

failed_attempts:
· exact h₁
      · exact h₂
    exact h₃

by
    have h₁ : AEMeasurable G P := (hasLaw_iff _).1 hG |>.2
    have h₂ : AEMeasurable H P := (hasLaw_iff _).1 hY |>.2
    -- Use the fact that the infimum of two measurable functions is measurable
    have h₃ : AEMeasurable (fun ω ↦ G ω ⊓ H ω) P := by
      refine (AEMeasurable.prod h₁ h₂).inferInstance
    exact h₃

by
    have h₁ : AEMeasurable G P := by
      have h₂ : Measurable G := by
        -- Prove that G is measurable
        have h₃ : Measurable G := by
          -- Use the fact that G is a function from a measurable space to a measurable space
          have h₄ : Measurable G := by
            -- Use the fact that G is a function from a measurable space to a measurable space
            simp_all [IsBinomialRandom, HasLaw]
            <;>
            (try simp_all [HasLaw])
            <;>
            (try simp_all [measurable_iff_adj])
            <;>
            (try simp_all [measurable_pi_iff])
            <;>
            (try simp_all [measurable_const])
            <;>
            (try simp_all [measurable_id])
            <;>
            (try simp_all [measurable_inf])
          exact h₄
        exact h₃
      exact h₂.aeMeasurable
    have h₂ : AEMeasurable H P := by
      have h₃ : Measurable H := by
        -- Prove that H is measurable
        have h₄ : Measurable H := by
          -- Use the fact that H is a function from a measurable space to a measurable space
          simp_all [IsBinomialRandom, HasLaw]
          <;>
          (try simp_all [HasLaw])
          <;>
          (try simp_all [measurable_iff_adj])
          <;>
          (try simp_all [measurable_pi_iff])
          <;>
          (try simp_all [measurable_const])
          <;>
          (try simp_all [measurable_id])
          <;>
          (try simp_all [measurable_inf])
        exact h₄
      exact h₃.aeMeasurable
    have h₃ : AEMeasurable (fun ω => G ω ⊓ H ω) P := by
      -- Use the fact that the infimum of two measurable functions is measurable
      refine' (AEMeasurable.inf h₁ h₂)
    exact h₃

by
      have h5 : AEMeasurable (fun ω : Ω => (G ω, H ω)) P := by
        -- The product of two measurable functions is measurable
        have h6 : AEMeasurable G P := h2
        have h7 : AEMeasurable H P := h3
        exact AEMeasurable.prod_mk h6 h7
      -- The infimum of two simple graphs is measurable
      have h8 : AEMeasurable (fun x : SimpleGraph V × SimpleGraph V => x.1 ⊓ x.2) (volume) := by
        -- The infimum operation is measurable in the product space
        have h9 : Measurable (fun x : SimpleGraph V × SimpleGraph V => x.1 ⊓ x.2) := by
          -- Use the fact that all operations on simple graphs are measurable
          have h10 : Measurable (fun x : SimpleGraph V × SimpleGraph V => (x.1 ⊓ x.2).adj) := by
            -- The adjacency function of the infimum is the product of the adjacency functions
            simp [SimpleGraph.mem_adj, SimpleGraph.inf_adj]
            <;>
            circular {
              <;>
              (try
                simp_all [measurable_iff_adjoint, measurable_comap, measurable_const_prod,
                  measurable_id, adjoint_measurable, product_measurable, and_measurable,
                  left_unique, right_unique])
              <;>
              (try
                simp_all [adjoint_measurable, product_measurable, and_measurable, left_unique,
                  right_unique])
              <;>
              (try
                circular)
            }
          -- Use the fact that the adjacency function is measurable to deduce that the graph is measurable
          have h11 : Measurable (fun x : SimpleGraph V × SimpleGraph V => x.1 ⊓ x.2) :=
            by
              apply measurable_of_forall_adj
              intro u v
              -- The adjacency of the infimum is the product of the adjacencies
              simp [SimpleGraph.mem_adj, SimpleGraph.inf_adj]
              <;>
              circular {
                <;>
                (try
                  simp_all [measurable_iff_adjoint, measurable_comap, measurable_const_prod,
                    measurable_id, adjoint_measurable, product_measurable, and_measurable,
                    left_unique, right_unique])
                <;>
                (try
                  simp_all [adjoint_measurable, product_measurable, and_measurable, left_unique,
                    right_unique])
                <;>
                (try
                  circular)
              }
            <;>
            aesop
          exact h11
        -- Since the function is measurable, it is almost everywhere measurable
        exact aemeasurable_of_measurable h9
      -- The infimum of G and H is measurable because it is the composition of two measurable functions
      have h10 : AEMeasurable (fun ω : Ω => G ω ⊓ H ω) P := by
        -- Use the fact that the product of two almost everywhere measurable functions is almost everywhere measurable
        have h11 : AEMeasurable (fun ω : Ω => (G ω, H ω)) P := h5
        have h12 : AEMeasurable (fun x : SimpleGraph V × SimpleGraph V => x.1 ⊓ x.2) (volume) := h8
        -- The infimum of G and H is the composition of the product function and the infimum function
        have h13 : (fun ω : Ω => G ω ⊓ H ω) = (fun x : SimpleGraph V × SimpleGraph V => x.1 ⊓ x.2) ∘ (fun ω : Ω => (G ω, H ω)) := by
          funext ω
          <;>
          simp
        rw [h13]
        -- The composition of two almost everywhere measurable functions is almost everywhere measurable
        exact AEMeasurable.comp (by infer_instance) h12 h11
      exact h10
    exact h4
  exact h1
